Got both datasets. Here's the comparison:

## Orders (2026-02-09 to 2026-02-15)

2 orders found:

| Order | Customer | Date | SKU_A | SKU_B | SKU_C |
|-------|----------|------|-------|-------|-------|
| ord_1001 | Acme Co | 2026-02-10 | 3 | 1 | — |
| ord_1002 | Globex | 2026-02-14 | 2 | — | 4 |
| **Total needed** | | | **5** | **1** | **4** |

## Inventory vs. Demand

| SKU | Available | Needed | Surplus / Shortfall |
|-----|-----------|--------|---------------------|
| SKU_A | 10 | 5 | +5 |
| SKU_B | 1 | 1 | 0 (exact) |
| SKU_C | 2 | 4 | **-2 (SHORT)** |

## Result

**No — current inventory cannot fulfill all orders.** SKU_C is short by 2 units. Order `ord_1002` (Globex, 2026-02-14) requires 4 units of SKU_C but only 2 are available. SKU_A and SKU_B have sufficient stock.
